title: Chalamet or Jordan? Oscars Betting Becoming a $100 Million-Plus Business With Kalshi and Polymarket
text: Oscars betting and prediction markets have become a significant business, with Kalshi and Polymarket each facilitating hundreds of millions in annual trading across entertainment markets. For the 2026 Oscars, the Best Picture race between One Battle After Another and Sinners drove substantial trading volume. Over 100 million dollars was wagered across prediction markets on the 2026 Oscars. One Battle After Another maintained steady odds as the favorite throughout most of the season, but Sinners' momentum from the SAG Awards briefly created uncertainty. Prediction markets showed One Battle After Another at around 75-77% probability in the final days before the ceremony. The vibrancy of prediction markets reflects broader interest in the Oscars as both a cultural event and a betting opportunity. Traders can hedge bets across categories, with Best Picture being the most heavily traded contract. The accuracy of these markets has improved as digital platforms have made trading more accessible. Michael B. Jordan's strong performance in Sinners was reflected in betting odds for Best Actor, where he began as an underdog but gained momentum. The intersection of award predictions and betting has created new industry dynamics, with professional bettors employing statistical models alongside traditional awards tracking.
